== Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしやきふ}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしやきふ}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしやきふ}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てちからおしんしやきふ}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]

|名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]

{'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02vq6mf [[EN:Tejikarao Shrine]] [[FR:Tejikarao-jinja]] [[JA:手力雄神社 (岐阜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3517214}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]

Shrine|fr|Tejikarao-jinja|ja|手力雄神社 (岐阜市)}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Tejikarao Shrine |画像=[[File:Tejikara-O-jinjya Gifu2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Kuramae 6-8-22 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|44.5|N|136|48|19.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} |社格=former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 (860) |本殿= |例祭=2nd Saturday of April / October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nakagawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tejikarao Shrine''' (てぢからおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. It is regarded as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} Hinasumori Shrine of Atsumi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, though there is also the view identifying {{ill|lt=Hinasumori Shrine|Q11547446|WD=Q11547446|1=Hinamori Shrine}} in the same city. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in Jōgan 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}){{Cite web|ja|title=Gifu City Plan for Maintaining and Improving Historical Scenic Character|publisher=Gifu City|url=https://www.city.gifu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/568/keikaku2r40331_s.pdf|accessdate=2023-02-04|page=76}}. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|WD=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} (prelude: {{ill|lt=Battle of Komeno|Q11604415|WD=Q11604415|1=Battle of Komeno}}), fighting in the area burned most of the precincts. Main sanctuary rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1616|Q6788|WD=Q6788|1=1616}} (Genna 2), heiden in 1620, and heiden/worship hall in 1623. Later rebuilt again in Meiji era. In early Meiji, anti-Buddhist measures led to disposal of Buddhist images and implements. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} it became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. On Oct. 18, {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}}, designated {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office. === Precincts and surroundings === * Main sanctuary / heiden / worship hall * Shrine office * First torii * Second torii (stone) * Third torii (vermilion) === Main rites === Spring grand festival is famous as a “fire festival” where men carry a mikoshi loaded with gunpowder and fireworks through the precincts while showered with sparks. It has over 300 years of history and is designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/gifusi/tejikarahimaturi.html |title=Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}. === Major annual events === ; Grand festivals * Feb 22: Kinen-sai, Kayuzue rite * 2nd Saturday of April: Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=Tejikara no Himatsuri|Q11496921|WD=Q11496921|1=Tejikara Fire Festival}}) * Oct 22: Autumn grand festival * Nov 22: Niiname-sai ; Middle festivals * Jan 1: Saitan-sai * Jan 3: Genshi-sai * Feb 11: Kigensetsu-sai * Feb 23: Tenchōsetsu-sai * Dec 9: Chikyūsetsu-sai ; Minor festivals * Jul 14: Summer festival * 2nd Saturday of Oct: Memorial rite for war monument * Nov 15: Shichi-go-san * Dec 31: New Year’s Eve festival * 1st and 15th monthly: Tsukinami-sai === Cultural properties === ; Gifu Prefecture designated * Tejikarao Shrine Fire Festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ; Gifu City designated * Wooden monk-form deity statue (sculpture) * Sacred sake jar (craft) === Access === * About 5 min south on foot from {{ill|lt=Tejikara Station|Q7695230|WD=Q7695230|1=Tejikara Station}} (Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line) === Bibliography === * Akira Kimura (ed.), 'Tour of Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture' (1997) * Tejikarao Shrine (ed.), '1150th Anniversary Reconstruction Commemorative Volume' (2011) === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)|Q134888254|WD=Q134888254|1=Tejikarao Shrine (Ōgaki)}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=951&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8A%9B%E9%9B%84%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tejikarao Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tejikarao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]
